title: Notion OAuth Integration for User Authentication
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want to connect my Notion workspace via OAuth so that I can export data to Notion using my own credentials instead of a static token.
description:
Replace the existing static-token Notion integration with a proper OAuth flow. Users will authenticate with their Notion account through the standard OAuth consent screen, allowing the app to request scoped access to their Notion workspace.

### Implementation
- Register the DataTools Pro app with Notion as an OAuth integration
- Add a Notion settings page at `/user/notion` (modeled after the existing Tableau settings page)
- When a user first attempts to export to Notion, redirect them to the settings page if no token exists
- The OAuth callback URL is `/callback/notion`
- On successful authentication, display a visual indicator that Notion is connected
- Include a help section with video and documentation links on the settings page

### OAuth Flow
1. User navigates to Notion settings or clicks export-to-Notion
2. If no token, redirect to `/user/notion` settings page
3. User clicks "Connect to Notion" button, triggering the OAuth consent flow
4. Notion redirects back to `/callback/notion` with an authorization code
5. App exchanges code for access token and stores it
6. Settings page shows connected status
acceptance_criteria: User can initiate Notion OAuth from the Notion settings page
OAuth callback correctly exchanges the authorization code for an access token
Access token is stored securely per user account
Notion settings page shows connected/disconnected status
If no token exists, export-to-Notion redirects user to the settings page
Settings page layout matches the Tableau settings page pattern
Local development callback URL is supported for testing
ux_changes: New Notion settings page at /user/notion with connect button, connection status indicator, and help content (video + documentation links). Layout mirrors the existing Tableau settings page.
middleware_changes: OAuth flow handler: initiates authorization request, processes callback, exchanges code for token. Token validation on page load to determine connected status.
data_model_changes: Store Notion OAuth access token per user account.
api_changes: New routes: GET /user/notion (settings page), GET /callback/notion (OAuth callback handler).
implementation_notes: The OAuth app was registered with Notion and credentials shared via 1Password. A local callback URL (https://dtp.test/callback/notion) was added for development testing. The settings page currently uses placeholder Tableau video and documentation links that need to be replaced with Notion-specific content.
comment_summary: Developer built the OAuth flow and Notion settings page modeled after the Tableau settings page. A local callback URL was needed for testing. The page was deployed to development for review. Video and documentation links still need to be updated from Tableau placeholders to Notion-specific content.
